note: End-to-end pipeline: victim swap -> EscrowRegistered -> post-audit (RPC + LLM, ProtectedSwapEscrowed decoded with output_amount vs expected_output) -> protected_swap_output_shortfall rule signal -> severity threshold mapping -> AuditDecision -> insurance settlement. Normal swap should land in info/RELEASE; sandwich victim swap should land in high|critical/BLOCK_AND_CLAIM with insurance pool refunding the user's input principal.

severityToActionPolicy.medium: RELEASE (with stderr warning; prod should require human review)

audit.overall_summary: The transaction executed a protectedExactInputSingle swap, sending 100.5 USDT from the subject address and receiving 99.44 AEGIS, with no risky conditions detected.

The transaction performed a protected swap of 100 USDT expecting 99 AEGIS, but only 73.15 AEGIS was received, resulting in a 26.11% shortfall and indicating a potential adverse price impact or sandwich attack.

vulnerabilities.title: Protected Swap Output Shortfall
vulnerabilities.summary: The protected swap escrow emitted an output of 73.146657748428651291 AEGIS against the user-stated expected output of 99 AEGIS, a shortfall of 26.11%. This discrepancy suggests that the swap was executed under unfavorable market conditions, possibly due to sandwiching, MEV, or other price manipulation, exposing the user to financial loss.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement stricter slippage protection or limit orders for protected swaps, verify on-chain price data before execution, and consider using price oracles with time-weighted averages. Users should also monitor for abnormal price impact and avoid large swaps in low-liquidity pools.
